2025-06-27.abstract: Susan Namangale fell in love with the game at age 9 in her small village, and she’s now on a mission to deliver a message to the whole country: Chess is good for everyone.

2025-06-27.title: A Runestone That May Be North America’s Oldest Turns Up in a Canada Forest
2025-06-27.abstract: Researchers spent years quietly studying a stone carved with 255 runes and the image of a boat found in Ontario. Now, revealing the stone’s existence, they’re asking the public for help.

2025-06-28.title: What Visual and Audio Evidence Reveals About the Air India Crash
2025-06-28.abstract: Photos, videos and flight tracking data reveal what happened before the flight crashed less than a minute after taking off.

2025-06-28.title: Chinese Police Detain Dozens of Writers Over Gay Erotic Online Novels
2025-06-28.abstract: The genre known as Boys’ Love, stories written mostly by and for straight women, has been in the authorities’ sights for years.

2025-06-28.title: Budapest Pride Parade Was Bigger Than Ever, Despite Orban’s Ban
2025-06-28.abstract: Prime Minister Viktor Orban’s party enacted the ban, but Budapest’s mayor allowed the event to go on. The police sat on the sidelines.

2025-06-28.title: Judgments Are Sharply Divided on Pope Leo’s Handling of Sex Abuse Cases
2025-06-28.abstract: For some survivors, he was a hero. Others who reported abuse said he failed them.

2025-06-28.title: Canada’s Trump-Fueled Brain Gain
2025-06-28.abstract: The University of Toronto has attracted several U.S. professors amid turmoil between American higher-education institutions and the Trump administration.

2025-06-29.title: How Europe Got Tough on Migration
2025-06-29.abstract: The European Union has not gone as far as President Trump in cracking down on immigration, but its shift is already profound.

2025-06-29.title: For South Koreans,‘Squid Game’ Was More Than Just Entertainment
2025-06-29.abstract: The Netflix hit has left a lasting legacy as a major cultural export, but also one that exposed some unsettling aspects of Korean society.

2025-06-29.title: The Beatboxing, Dancing Nuns Expanding the Flock in Brazil
2025-06-29.abstract: The Catholic Church’s effort to win young followers relies in part on influencers, D.J.s and two Brazilian nuns with plenty of rhythm.

2025-06-29.title: After War With Israel and U.S., Iran Rests on a Knife Edge
2025-06-29.abstract: The Islamic Republic limps on after the 12-day conflict. Where will the nation go from here?

2025-06-29.title: Refugees Worry: Is Nicaragua Assassinating Government Critics in Costa Rica?
2025-06-29.abstract: After a Nicaraguan human rights activist who had fled to Costa Rica was killed, concern has grown that the Ortega government may be targeting its enemies abroad.

2025-06-30.title: Canada Will Scrap Tax That Prompted Trump to Suspend Trade Talks
2025-06-30.abstract: The government said on Sunday night that it would cancel its tax on American technology companies, handing a victory to the Trump administration.

2025-06-30.title: They Demanded Democracy. Years Later, They Are Still Paying the Price.
2025-06-30.abstract: Thousands of young people lost careers, friends and dreams after taking part in mass antigovernment protests that erupted six years ago in Hong Kong.

2025-06-30.title: Drifting From the West’s Orbit, Russians Find a New Role Model in China
2025-06-30.abstract: China has become trendy for Russians who once worshiped everything Western. Young people are learning Mandarin, and Chinese culture and goods have become ubiquitous in Moscow.

2025-06-30.title: The War Within the Sinaloa Cartel Explained
2025-06-30.abstract: One of the world’s most powerful criminal syndicates is facing a government crackdown and internal war after the son of a drug lord betrayed his partner.

2025-06-30.title: Cartel Fighters Make a Desperate Alliance That Could Transform Underworld
2025-06-30.abstract: The Sinaloa Cartel, the world’s most-feared fentanyl trafficker, is reeling from an internal war and a U.S.-Mexican crackdown. Its fate could upend global criminal networks.
